issues.title: SRP: Form validation logic embedded directly in component
issues.description: SellerCheckoutCard.handleSubmit contains inline required-field validation logic (lines 87-97) that iterates form fields and constructs error messages. This is application/domain logic living in a presentation component. If validation rules change (e.g. adding min-length checks, regex patterns, or translating field labels properly) the component must change for a non-UI reason.
issues.fix: Extract field validation to a pure utility function in application/ or domain/ layer: e.g. `validateFormFields(fields, buyerSubmission): string | null`. The component then just calls it and sets the error state.

issues.title: SRP + i18n: Hardcoded English string in validation — `Please fill in: ${field.label_en}`
issues.description: Line 93 constructs a validation error with a hardcoded English prefix and uses `field.label_en` directly. This is a presentation concern mixed with business validation and bypasses the i18n system. An eslint-disable comment already acknowledges the issue. The label is dynamic user data, but the surrounding sentence `Please fill in:` is UI copy that should go through `useTranslations`.
issues.fix: Pass a translation function or a pre-translated template string into the validator, or move the error formatting into the component after the validator returns which field failed.

issues.title: OCP: if/type chain for rendering different field inputs
issues.description: The `renderInput()` function at line 39 uses if/if/return to switch on `field.type` ('textarea', 'file', default). Adding a new field type (e.g. 'select', 'checkbox') requires modifying this function. The pattern is straightforward and the number of types is small, but it is a textbook OCP tension point.
issues.fix: Consider a renderer map: `const FIELD_RENDERERS: Record<string, (props) => JSX.Element> = { textarea: ..., file: ..., }`. New types extend the map without touching existing code. Given KISS, only apply if types are growing or the component is frequently changed.

issues.title: SRP: `downloadExcel` mixes file generation concern with DOM browser side-effect
issues.description: The file exports both `exportSellerOrdersToExcel` (pure XML/string generation) and `downloadExcel` (DOM manipulation to trigger a browser download). The XML builder is a pure function and could be tested in isolation. The `downloadExcel` function is a side-effectful browser utility that accesses `document`. Having them in the same file means testing the pure builder requires a DOM environment. This is a minor SRP concern — the file is small and cohesive around the export feature.
issues.fix: Optionally move `downloadExcel` to a shared browser utility (e.g. `shared/application/utils/downloadFile.ts`) if the download trigger pattern is needed elsewhere. Low priority given KISS — current co-location is acceptable for a feature-specific utility.

issues.title: OCP: APP_ACCESS_RULES is an open configuration map — well-structured
issues.description: AppNavigation uses a `APP_ACCESS_RULES` configuration object (lines 48-74) to determine visibility per app, with a `matchesPermissions` function handling the evaluation. Adding a new app with access rules only requires adding an entry to the map. No conditional logic needs modification. This is a good application of OCP through configuration. Recording as Info/positive finding.
issues.fix: No action needed. This is a well-applied OCP pattern.

issues.title: ISP: Props decomposed into focused sub-interfaces — well-structured
issues.description: SellerCheckoutContent decomposes its props into `SubmissionState`, `MethodSelectionState`, and `BuyerFormState` interfaces rather than a single flat prop object. Each group represents a distinct concern. Components that would compose this (like SellerCheckoutCard) construct these objects explicitly. This is a textbook correct application of ISP. Recording as positive finding.
